text: The surgeon gains primary laparoscopic access through the right iliac fossa port due to the patient's umbilical hernia. The omentum is visible herniating through the umbilical defect, and it is carefully taken down from the hernial sac before inserting the umbilical port.

The surgeon retracts the sigmoid colon ventrally and incises the medial peritoneal fold, using a medial to lateral approach to dissect in the avascular plane while avoiding injury to the left ureter. The inferior mesenteric artery is dissected, clipped, and divided at its origin, followed by division of the inferior mesenteric vein. The lateral peritoneal fold is incised, and dissection continues cranially to mobilize the splenic flexure. Major dissection occurs in the avascular presacral plane, extending to the pelvic floor.

The surgeon makes a lateral peritoneal incision in the pelvis and skeletonizes the fundibulopelvic ligament, which is then clipped and divided. The paravesical and pararectal spaces are developed, revealing the origin of the uterine artery and vein and their relation to the ureter. The uterine artery is dissected off the ureter and clipped at its origin from the internal iliac artery. The ureter is further dissected and completely mobilized up to its insertion into the bladder.

The surgeon clips and divides the round ligament, repeating the procedure on the right side. An incision is made on the uterovisceral fold of the peritoneum, separating the bladder from the lower uterine segment and cervix. Dissection is performed circumferentially over the McCartney tube, detaching the posterior vagina from the rectal wall.

The surgeon continues lateral dissection in the pararectal space, freeing the rectum from surrounding tissues up to the levator ani muscle. The specimen is transected after total mesorectal excision using an endostapler and is retrieved through the umbilical hernia defect following a mini-laparotomy. Vaginal closure is performed using V-lock sutures.
The surgeon performs an end-to-end coloanal anastomosis using a circular stapler. The incisional hernia is closed, and an overlay proline mesh is placed. A diversion loop ileostomy is created.
